article_context: This body image supports the first tip and its caption: the cat’s relaxed, unhesitating use of the litter tray is presented as evidence that the chosen litter is comfortable and acceptable to the pet.
base_scene: A calm cat is already midway through stepping out of the litter tray. It continues the same confident movement until all four paws are on the floor, reinforcing the idea that comfortable, natural behavior indicates acceptance of the litter.
image_reading: A gray tabby cat is poised on the rim of a litter tray, with one front paw already planted on the wooden floor and the other reaching forward.
The cat’s body leans toward screen right, creating a clear path for a natural, continuous exit from the tray.
The visible movable details are the cat’s legs, shifting torso, head, ears, tail, and whiskers; the surrounding furniture and houseplant can remain still.

models.positive_prompt: The cat calmly steps forward out of the litter tray, placing each paw naturally on the floor as its weight shifts smoothly off the rim. Its head stays alert and turns slightly toward screen right, with subtle ear and tail movement. Keep the camera fixed, ending with the cat fully on the floor and continuing one gentle step forward.
models.scene_plan: Over the five-second fixed-camera shot, the cat steadily completes its exit rather than making a single quick step: it transfers weight onto the planted front paw, lowers the second front paw, brings each hind leg over the rim, and finishes fully on the floor with one gentle forward step. Small head, ear, and tail adjustments accompany the same movement, and the readable final state arrives near the end.
